760414: South Korea pulled off the classic "we're gonna miss everything and then suddenly remember how to score" move, coming back from down 1-0 to beat Czechia 2-1 with a late goal from Oh Hyeon-Gu in the 79th minute. Son Heung-Min was out here firing shots into orbit all game while the Czechs basically showed up, scored once on their only decent chance, and then got immediately humbled by Hwang In-Beom's equalizer.
760415: Mexico finally broke their cursed opening-game streak by absolutely bullying South Africa 2-0 at home, with Quiñones scoring in the ninth minute after a turnover and Raúl Jiménez adding the second—though the real entertainment was watching South Africa implode down to nine men with red cards flying around like confetti. Basically, Mexico won so hard they couldn't even enjoy it because their own guy got sent off at the end, which is peak Mexico energy honestly.
760416: Canada finally got their first home World Cup goal thanks to Cyle Larin coming off the bench in the 78th minute, but Bosnia-Herzegovina had already gone up 1-0 off a Jovo Lukic header in the first half, so the whole thing ended in a draw in front of Connor McDavid and Ryan Reynolds (yes, *that* Ryan Reynolds). The real drama is that Canada's only scored 2 goals total across three World Cup appearances, so getting one at home felt like winning the lottery, even if they couldn't actually finish it.
